reviews.review_text:
This holiday park was pure bliss. You really get the best of both worlds — the surrounding terrain is incredible, the creek is safe for kids, and the views feel almost surreal. Then, just a short walk across the road, you’re at the coastline along the Great Ocean Road, with plenty of waves and great spots to fish off the rocks.

The staff were friendly, and the riverview cabins were clean and in a fantastic location. A few areas could do with some upgrades, and it was a bit disappointing that the trail had been damaged by the January storms. But overall, we’ll definitely be coming back.

We’ve visited heaps of times and the location is beautiful, but the policies here have gotten out of hand. We were charged extra for three adults, denied a refund on a cancelled night, and when they owed us money on a fee discrepancy, we were told to spend it at their overpriced store. Rigid and inflexible — not what you expect from a caravan park. They’ve lost us as regulars.

Had an absolutely wonderful time here. The first night we had there was no other campers there, and there was horrible 55km winds.. the staff came to check on us and asked if we needed some extra tent pegs. Once the weather cleared up we had the most amazing time. Wildlife was a highlight, the grounds are very well maintained and we found ourselves very fond of the staff members as we saw them cleaning the grills and bathrooms regularly. Top tier place to stay for some downtime from work and the internet. The laundry room and dishwashing area was greatly appreciated, and with just a 10 minute drive to Lorne it was well worth the higher price point. Highly recommend.
Labai graži vieta, būtinai rekomenduoju apsistoti šitam kempinge.
Good toilet and shower nice people ! And amazing view

What a beautiful part of the Great Ocean Road. I’m so glad that the park is nearly back to normal after the devastating flood. We absolutely loved our 2 night stay. All the staff were amazing and made us feel so welcome. We’ll definitely come back for a longer stay next time. Definitely one of the highlights of our trip along the GOR.

Very strict policies regarding cleaning, fires, and check-in/check-out procedures, and unfortunately not very friendly staff.
When I arrived at reception to check in, the lady was on the phone for about five minutes without even acknowledging me or saying a word. A simple “I’ll be with you shortly” would have been courteous.
Very expensive for what you get, not enough amenities.
We decided to leave a day earlier due to the weather, and we were exiting around 1 pm. The boom gate would not open, and a man from reception approached and immediately said, “You are late to check out,” without even greeting us. Was he planning to lock us in if we were late?
I told him he should ask for my site number first, and explained that we were actually leaving earlier than planned, not late. He then opened the boom gate without saying anything further.
Very disappointing experience. I don’t think I’ll be returning unless there are changes to the overly strict policies and the overall attitude of the staff.

Best place to get away from the hustle and bustle of the city with family and friends. Perfect for a digital detox and reconnecting with nature. A truly beautiful place, and the staff are amazing friendly, helpful, and welcoming. I would definitely recommend this place, and we will surely come back.

Nice place in beautiful surroundings. However, there are too few toilets for the number of people vacationing there, which is also reflected in their condition – they get dirty very quickly. Additionally, there is only Wi-Fi at the BBQ hut. The problem is that many people gather there because there is no other network coverage on the site. As a result, you keep getting disconnected from the Wi-Fi, or it becomes extremely slow. For the campsite price, one would expect better Wi-Fi throughout the facilities. Nevertheless, it’s a pleasant location and right by the beach.

Don't listen to the negative comments, it almost put me off booking, but I'm so glad we went for it anyway!

Good communication for the owners, we arrived late & they left us an envelope with instructions/Pitch number.

Site is so so beautiful, even when full! We had no issues with facilities, didn't queue for toilets or showers once and the place was sold out.

The beach opposite is gorgeous, we did swim & was lovely! Saw kangaroos on our first night here and the surrounding cliffs & river are just beautiful.

Would highly recommend!

Really disappointed with their communication. Booked a week. Arrived and spoke with office staff.  We then set up the caravan, but then the power didn’t work.  We noticed all the power boxes had signs saying they’re not working. Went back to office and was told that their generators aren’t working and they don’t know when they will be back on. Apparently, that information was posted on their website. But we went back on-line and couldn’t find any notice about having no power.  We then had to pack up the van and move to the Lorne foreshore caravan park.  We didn’t even get the full site costs returned to us.  Pretty disappointing.
Good palace to do camping
Great accomodating staffs. The location is perfect for a holiday experience.
A bit expensive

We stayed in a 2 bed cabin over the weekend.  Overpriced for what you get or should I say don’t get.  No toaster in the cabin and only 1 power outlet that only accommodates charging your phone. If you try to use it for anything else it cuts out. I get they are on a generator but perhaps installing solar panels on the roof would solve this issue. Aside from that this is a breathtaking place to stay. The river and the cliff face are beautiful to look at , on a clear night the stars are spectacular.
No mention of tent sites right in front of deluxe villa porch. You get to see people camping, not a clear view of nature that you had paid for. Loved the nature but not the service and facilities.
This place is my heaven on earth. Everything about it is amazing.

Rude and unhelpful staff, laughing and blatantly disregarding when a concern was raised. Failed to provide a refund after leaving the campground and not staying the night, considering the high price point for no water or power and poorly managed amenities I would advise all to steer clear and head to one of the other lovely spots nearby.

Best place to get away from Melbourne suburbs heaps of wildlife kangaroos , birds
Ocean across the road no power points in cabins they run of generators and no phone coverage only out on the great Ocean Rd

Couldn't recommend this place more! We had the best stay here recently. Service was exceptional and accommodation of a great standard the cabins and clean.
Best spot to stay and explore as a couple or young family.

I booked a cedar cabin.  I appreciated that the heater was on and the cabin was lovely and warm when I arrived.  It was very clean and cosy and comfortable.  The park was quiet  and every staff member  I met were friendly and happy. I recommend this park.  Bonus...friendly wildlife, kookaburras, kangaroos and too many others birds to list.
Tucked away by the river’s gentle flow, the Cumberland River Holiday Park is a true slice of paradise and surrounded by nature’s soothing embrace.

Excellent location if you want to get away and not be bothered by your phone etc. as there is no mobile reception within the site.|Facilities are quite basic with limited or no water & power. Friends satying in cabins werre not allowed hair dryers or toasters due to lack of power. Showers in the toilet block are timed and cut off automatically.|Customer service was poor with zero flexibility on number of cars or checkout times eventhough the place was almost empty during our stay. They are there to make money, not service their customers needs.

Very expensive considering there is no power and no water. Lucky we have a beautiful van so made it ok. Very inflexible. Wanted to charge an extra $10 for an extra hour late check out when really its a plot of dirt. Nothing for them to do. No cleaning or anything. We wanted to cook our dinner on BBQ's which they only have 2 of which is really ridiculous and only one was working. Thats very poor. The other one was used by a heap of bikies. So no dinner for us that night. Not good enough. Only 1 car per site but they allowed a heap of motorbikes at their cabin. Not fair. Will not return and would advise not to stay there.

The staff were extremely rude and unhelpful. Like, sarcastically rude & downright mean, in the face of an issue that was truly no ones fault and would have no bearing on their operations.... It totally ruined the vibe of such and idyllic location. Well done, it takes some really crappy service for me to write a review... we won't be returning and will be advising others to look elsewhere!

It’s a great location, very serene, and clean! Perfect for camping with friends!
Although the toilets were not the cleanest!
Great spot a little expensive  for no power or water but still a nice spot .
Great camping sites with no mobile phone coverage, digital detox!

Review: Unprofessional and Disrespectful Handling of Lost Property

I stayed at Cumberland River Caravan Park in October 2024 and my experience regarding their management of lost property has been appalling. After realising I had left behind an expensive cashmere shawl, I contacted the park within a couple of weeks. I was reassured that the item had been found and would be securely held for me.

At no point was I informed of any "three-month policy" regarding the holding of lost items. Had this been mentioned, I would have made immediate arrangements. When I phoned to say I would be making the long trip from the other end of Victoria to collect it personally, early 2025, I was abruptly told the item had been "disposed of."

If I was reassured my item would be kept, why was there no courtesy to contact or notify me before throwing it out? Particularly given the distance involved, this basic communication should have been the absolute minimum standard.

Frankly, I find it hard to believe that an expensive cashmere shawl was simply discarded. It raises serious concerns about whether items of value are genuinely "disposed of" or whether they are mishandled or even misappropriated. At best, this is gross incompetence; at worst, it borders on negligence and disrespect.

The complete lack of accountability, transparency, and professionalism is appalling. I placed my trust in the staff’s assurances and was let down at every point.

I will never return and strongly advise others to think twice before staying here if they value their belongings—or expect even the most basic decency in customer service.

Had the most amazing time! Saw the kangaroo's 🦘, all the beautiful bird wild life such as the Kookaburra, Cockatoo & Rosella's. The River & Rockwall is so sensational & calming to look at! The stars ✨ at night are sensational! Enjoyed a nice wine around the 🔥 fire pit, extremely relaxing! We also kept well feed with the gorgeous, clean BBQ facilities you have! Thank you so much for providing a peaceful, safe & relaxing atmosphere! This is our most favourite holiday destination! Appreciate you all being so accommodating to us & all of U looking after the park so well! See you again soon. Kindly EJ

If you are looking for a nice and peaceful place to go camping, do NOT choose this place. My partner and I enjoy camping to relax and get away and the trip to this campground was horrible to say the least. The campsites are shoulder to shoulder, not much space at all. The young people next to us had their music on until 2am and were screaming at the top of their lungs to the point where I was laying in the tent contemplating to leave. We told reception and they couldn’t have cared less if they tried. This place is ridiculous and their rules about no noise after 10pm is not truly followed, so if you’re looking for a good campsite to party on this is the one. Considering it is quite pricey I’m very suprised they do not enforce their rules. I would not recommend this place to anyone. It is a shame because the views are nice and it is in a great spot, just a very poorly run business. My partner and I had to pack up and leave the second night because they were once again loud and reception did nothing about it. Do not go here.

Beautiful views of a cliff and river. Great for kids to play in. Service is friendly and welcoming. There are fire pits located throughout the park. There are kangaroos wondering freely.

The negatives, sites are all unpowered. The toilets and showers are very basic. There are 2 x showers and 2 x toilets, be prepared to queue. 2 x communal sinks for dishes, again insufficient. Camp kitchen have 2 x bbq stoves, no fridge or shared tables / spaces.
A hidden gem. Could use power on the camping sites and some mobile reception though!

This is a hidden gem nestled between the ranges in the Great Otway National Park on the Great ocean road just before Lorne . Had a 3 nites stay in our swag camped next to the Cumberland river . No wifi 😄 , babbling creek and wildlife everywhere. Serenity now. Camp grounds were very well maintained and staff were very friendly and helpful. Thank you

The real thing. Unhooked from the rest of the world, bbq along the river among animals and beautiful nature. Very nice and helpful people and clean facilities. Not a very big camping, so making a reservation is advisable.  An absolute should do.

It’s a beautiful place, but unfortunately, we had the worst experience ever. There were too many young people staying there, and they made noise even midnight. We couldn’t sleep for two nights—I felt like I was sleeping in a pub. There was also too much swearing, which was really bad for the kids. Such a shame!

Had a cabin, was quiet till a laud group arrived , drunks chatter over whole campground...couldn't sleep much last night heard them through closed windows till late night.
Otherwise great place, amazing hikes ,or walks , big lonely beach.

Beautiful location but that’s about it. For up to $85 for a non powered no water hookup site you get bugger all else. Amenities are basic. Two (rarely cleaned) toilets and two showers for potentially several hundred campers. No camp kitchen but there is a free electric bbq. After a busy weekend the dumpster bins are overflowing with rubbish. Latecomers weren’t able to access the park even though the manager drove in at the same time. Could easily have let these two in and asked them to pay in the morning. Just mean spirited. And the park was virtually empty in any case! Won’t be staying here again and will be warning people off. For the price I would expect a whole lot more! DO NOT STAY HERE.

Beautiful location but that’s about it. For up to $85 for a non powered no water hookup site you get bugger all else. Amenities are basic. Two (rarely cleaned) toilets and two showers for potentially several hundred campers. No camp kitchen but there is a free electric bbq. After a busy weekend the dumpster bins are overflowing with rubbish. Latecomers weren’t able to access the park even though the manager drove in at the same time. Could easily have let these two in and asked them to pay in the morning. Just mean spirited. And the park was virtually empty in any case! Won’t be staying here again and will be warning people off. For the price I would expect a whole lot more! DO NOT STAY HERE.

I camped during peak season so can't comment on the rooms. I called beforehand to let them know that I would be late (after hours) and the staff were so lovely in how they accommodated me. They are impressively professional, organised and maintain the campgrounds with dedicated ground staff. One of them brought leads and a battery for a jump-start. Thank you! It was quiet and relaxed in a beautiful setting. All campers were respectful and I was fortunate to have super friendly neighbours to boot.

I read one review that they may cull some wildlife, I hope this remains a rumour because wildlife is an integral part of camping. If I wanted a wildlife free camp, I'd go to one of the big franchises and I would rate this campground commensurately. You have a point of difference so keep up your classy act, Cumberland!
One of the best, amazing park.

We stayed for a long weekend looking for a nice camping spot near the sea. Ram, Charlotte and the team on site were amazing and friendly from check-in to check out. Site was well maintained and the location, scenery and wildlife were wonderful. Thanks team, we will be back.

I love our stay here! The nature and serenity are such a vibe. Plus, the bathroom smells nice! I wonder what room spray they use. If you’re going to stay here, just watch out for the kookaburras—I got swooped at twice while eating my BBQ and ended up having to eat in our tent hahaha. The staff is very nice and welcoming, too! I will definitely come back here!

Great spots along the river with a short walk to the beach. Perfect for families and only just out of Lorne. No mobile reception and need to be fully self contained. Staff were very accommodating. Can have a fire.

A great location for nature lovers! This place is close to the beach and a beautiful creek, making it perfect for a relaxing getaway. We stayed for two nights in a 3-bedroom cabin, which was clean and tidy. They provided all the necessary washroom supplies and bedding, which was very convenient. Just a heads up, since this is a camping site, you may not be able to use your mobile phone or high-voltage electrical items easily. Also, it's important to double-check the weather before booking, as you could end up staying during a windy or rainy spell. Overall, a fantastic spot if you love nature and a bit of adventure!
Amazing place to get with the family.

The 3 stars are for the location which is beautiful. The staff member was friendly and helpful. The negatives for us outweigh the location. There are 2 very small toilet blocks. There were 3 toilets including one disabled in our nearest block and 2 showers. The block was in really bad condition, rotting wood, dodgy looking roof full of spider webs and no hand dryer or paper towel. We were lucky to be there on a quiet night with no-one nearby. In our riverview sandy gravel tent area there are about 27 tent sites, you can have 6 people in each site. This area isn't huge. Behind the riverview tent area are lots of various tent and caravan areas probably around 40+ plus cabins. Even half full there aren't enough toilet and shower facilities. I can't imagine this would be a pleasant experience. Add in the closeness of your neighbours hopefully you get good ones not the ones who play music to entertain the whole area it would be awful. We didn't look at the second smaller toilet block but can't imagine it's modern, clean with heaps of showers and toilets. This place needs some serious work.

If there as a zero they’d be getting that!
I can’t believe how rude some people are.
Not only were they rude, unprofessional, they also have ZERO remorse or compassion for humanity!
The day before we were meant to leave, our world was thrown upside with a parent being told they’re palliative with a week or so left to live. As soon as we were told this, we called the accomodation apologising profoundly for having to cancel.
WHO IN THEIR RIGHT MIND WOULD GO ON A HOLIDAY AND LEAVE THEIR PARENT TO DIE ALONE? 🤦🏻‍♀️
so.. yeah thanks but NO THANKS. Will never book here again. And we will tell anyone who listens about our experience! Trust me, we know a lot of people, and our community is massive!
$1125 they just nicely “earnt” , with a “bad luck” that’s out policy and “if you don’t want to lose your money get family or friends to take over your accomodation”.
Wow!
Congrats to all the other places around them, you’re about to gain a whole lot of business!

Booked for 4 nights and paid up front. The day before we were supposed to leave we had a family emergency with terrible news. I rang to postpone our cabins and was told no and no refunds either. No compassion at all. Will never go back
Awesome  place
Great low key campsites and cabins on the river with hikes to Jebs pool and waterfalls

My partner and I are currently touring around Victoria, this was our first place we stayed at when we got here. It was the perfect taste of Victoria and the Great Ocean Road, a stunning location right next to the river but also the ocean. It had nice and clean ammenities and a really relaxed feel about it. We were also greeted by many ducks and also some kangaroo in the evening which was a highlight for me also. Highly recommend this place, definitely one of my favourites so far!

We had planned to come and stay here for a weekend trip next month but we have just heard that they are going to SHOOT some of the wildife who live at the property. We queried this and got an email from the management committee confirming that they’re hiring someone to come and KILL the beautiful native birds. We can’t support them now that we know this. There are plenty of options in Lorne who operate in kind and more sustainable ways.
Recommended to all Campers.100% you Love it...😉

After calling to rebook a few days out after we had an emergency they told us no refunds and we could not use it again another time.|Rude and forward over the phone.|Over priced with no power.|Would recommend other parks close by as it seems they are not a family friendly park but only using location to make prices for sites which are ridiculously high.

Fly fishing for Trout on one of the better Otway streams ✌️👍
Photo’s to follow.

We were booked for 4 nights in the campground however due to extreme winds which damaged the canvas on our camper we had to pack up and leave after just 1 night.  We had read that there were no refunds however were very disappointed that the Cumberland River Holiday Park policy doesn’t have any flexibility for exceptional circumstances.  We won’t be staying again and I recommend against booking ahead for more than 1 night.

Loved the place! There is a stunning rock pool if you go on small 20-25 minutes hike, not a place toddlers can go as you need to cross the river by hopping on rocks to get there, but can be easily done with kids over 4-5 years , its a 5-10 minute walk to the beach
We loved our stay, keep in mind its a unpowered site

Only reason im giving 4* instead of 5* is because the bathrooms/ toilets are very very dated. If not its the perfect place, perfect spot

The best place I have ever been. My kids didn’t even want to come back home. Weekdays are the best days to book to get the max relaxation. Love the breath taking view and the wildlife.. can’t go wrong with this place. Looking forward to go back there again

Great experience with friends! 10 pm curfew. Minimal power outlet for cabins. Beautiful location near beach. Very nice, lake & walking path.
Will go back again.

Our stay at Cumberland river was excellent. The scenery is stunning and it was a very pleasant trip all round. One thing to mention is that the management has changed since last year, and they are absolutely lovely- very kind and accommodating. We would definitely stay here again- perfect for couples and families !! Thank you!
Great and clean location 👌🏻
Gorgeous location- nestled under a towering escarpment beside a picturesque little river. Walking access to the beach. Well kept facilities and friendly accommodating staff.

We stayed in the river cabins and had a lovely stay. The cabins were clean, had comfy beds outdoor area had chairs table and fire pit. The location is amazing and I actually loved having no wifi or reception. It was great to logoff and go and explore the amazing surroundings. Already planning our next visit.

Great campsite to be at in the summer, the views were stunning and the location was not to far from Lorne. The river was nice to swim in and the walk to jebbs pool from the site was lovely. The downsides were the facilities which were lacking and the toilets weren't the cleanest. Unfortunately you couldn't really swim in the nearby beach because of safety so quite a few trips into town were necessary.   The onsite kiosk was limited and very pricey but good for the convenience ($4 for a litre of milk).

My partner and I have stayed here a few times before, it’s a great place to stay.

Unfortunately today we booked the last remaining river site without realising the weather forecast had changed. It was now predicted for wind and rain the next 24hrs. 20mins after transferring our $55 deposit for the unpowered site I called back to see if they could offer a refund.

I was very disappointed to hear that management wouldn’t allow a refund or a credit. Even though all other sites were fully booked. I understand there is a same day cancellation policy. But in particular situations like this, where a honest human error has been made I think asking for a refund 20mins after booking wasn’t unreasonable.

We won’t be staying here again with the taste that’s been left in our mouths.

Great campground in a peaceful setting. Great location on a creek, easy walk to the beach and hiking trails. There’s no mobile phone reception and no powered sites, but in some ways that adds to the experience. The toilets/showers are clean with one old amenities block and one new one. Fires are allowed which is great because not many places allow that these days.

This is the worst ran park/business I have ever come across in my entire life. Be careful as they will charge your account multi times and claim you have not made payment. I have been over charged even emailed them all the proof the money is taken out of my account and paid to theirs and still no help with a resolution. Very poor form to rip someone off after they had already paid the 470 dollars for two nights accommodation. Would highly recommend staying away from here..

Clearly a beautiful spot, wonderful access to wildlife etc. However as stated by other reviewers, management is not only horrible to deal with but negligent in an emergency. I wish I had’ve done more research and seen the way they consistently deal with families in need of assistance. ||We are seasoned campers and have spent nights holding on to tents in the wind, we get that sometimes that is part of it. However on Tues Sept 19 the wind was so bad it came roaring into camp flattening everything in its path. Our kids were terrified and after dealing with it for a few hours, my young daughter started having issues breathing, we tried to manage this and holding up the tent but as it was around 11pm we felt we were getting into emergency territory. ||We abandoned the tent and headed for reception where we had seen the emergency button. At this stage we were managing her breathing with an asthma pump but it was hard to keep her calm in a wind storm with no real shelter. When the guy came out I explained our situation and wondered if he could help by providing some shelter temporarily. I was happy to pay whatever and the park was pretty empty at this stage. He was extremely defensive straight away and told me he had no access to keys and that he he didn’t control the weather. I was completely shocked. He’s a first responder, my kid was struggling to breathe, we had no viable shelter. When my partner justifiably questioned his role as an emergency responder, this guy ran at my partner with fists up. Ran at him. I have never seen anything like it. My partner stood his ground and informed him he would not be taking a step toward him. The guy bellowed something aggressively about how we should “call an Ambulance, call the police!”We had no phone reception. ||We got in the car, pretty shaken, and drove to town where we got her breathing under control in the car outside the hospital, ready to go in if need be. Meanwhile I called the Mantra in Lorne who we’re absolutely bloody amazing. They had a room ready for us in moments, they discounted it and gave us late check out to 12pm. They discounted breakfast, they welcomed us at every turn. The girl on the breakfast buffet offered to come help us with our tent after she got off her shift! I cannot speak more highly of their professionalism and kindness. They went above and beyond, so much so we ended up booking another night at the end of our trip, we were welcomed back like family. Honestly if you’re ever in Lorne spend a night at the Mantra!||We returned to our tent broken and soggy as the rain hit about 2am. My family were arriving (this was my birthday trip) the next day in cabins and there were strictly NO REFUNDS. We can’t afford to not go through with a trip over $1000 so we had to make the best of it. The woman who runs management helped us into a cabin for two nights and then I shared with my brother. For the remainder of the trip there were no apologies, the staff worked well enough to ensure we cobbled together a trip but the man in management with the anger issue left a very bad taste. ||It’s pretty at Cumberland River, the women know what they’re doing, but watch out if you need help. You may just get an unhinged man running at you in the middle of the night. That wasn’t taught in the first aid course, not that I can remember.

Was so disappointing - 4 days before our arrival i rang reception and queried the cancellation policy and the amount of money we would get back if we cancelled. "If". I was told that we would lose 1 night which was approx $300. I said to the receptionist that was fine and to "keep everything as It was". We ended up being cancelled and lost our accomodation for the weekend. The manager was very argumentative and wouldn't take responsibility for his staffs mistake. We ended up loosing the $300. |So so disappointing. |
You can experience a service that is secluded from the world.

Benim ve ailem için harika bir deneyim oldu. Tekrar mutlaka görüşeceğiz
Herşey için teşekkürler.
Harika bir yer Türk müşterilere tavsiye ederim ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
hatika bir yer

You enter the holiday park, kangaroos sleeping in the bushes, Cockatoos everywhere, and a Kookaburra was at our doorstep. Surrounded by trees and beautiful rock formations. You won't get reception for phones, good peace. Rooms are clean and has a full kitchen,  outside table and chairs and a fire pit. Nice place to chill .
Can’t get a refund even in the middle of winter for $35 5 days before the booking was for. That’s a joke
Great place to stop and enjoy the ocean csenery!
Absolutely stunning camping spot. Cannot wait to go back there again. No phone reception made it even better to switch off and get in touch with nature.

Cumberland River Holiday Park is the best place to have a quick getaway trip with friends. I booked a campsite for a large group to celebrate my birthday  - it was super easy to book, and the staff are so kind and accommodating, special thanks to Tamara for being so lovely and for making my stay so easy!
10/10 recommend coming here on any day!
